items.title: 3DOS Change the World
items.author: NGUYỄN CÔNG TRÁNG
items.content:
In a world that’s rapidly decentralizing — from finance to storage, and even social networks — one industry has remained largely untouched: manufacturing. That’s where 3DOS comes in.

3DOS is pioneering a new way to produce and distribute physical goods by combining the power of Web3, AI, and decentralized physical infrastructure (DePIN). It’s not just another blockchain project — it’s a movement to reshape global supply chains into something more resilient, transparent, and community-owned.

🚀 What is 3DOS? 3DOS is a decentralized manufacturing network built on the Sui blockchain. It connects makers, designers, and consumers through a “print-to-earn” economy. This means that anyone with a 3D printer — whether in Lagos, London, or Lahore — can join the network and start manufacturing products locally while earning rewards.

By decentralizing production, 3DOS reduces reliance on traditional, centralized factories and long-distance shipping. It empowers local communities to produce what they need, when they need it.

🌐 The Problem with Traditional Manufacturing Global supply chains are fragile. We've seen it with pandemic-induced shortages, geopolitical tensions, and environmental concerns. Current systems rely heavily on centralized factories, global logistics, and opaque middlemen.

High transportation costs

Massive carbon footprints

Lack of transparency in sourcing and production

And perhaps most importantly — manufacturing power stays in the hands of a few giant corporations.

🧠 How 3DOS + AI + DePIN Changes the Game By integrating AI and decentralized infrastructure, 3DOS is creating an “Internet of Production.” Here's how:

AI-powered browser extensions allow users to passively map supply and demand data in real-time.

DePIN (Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Networks) means that anyone can contribute their physical 3D printer or manufacturing setup to the network.

Smart contracts ensure fair rewards, quality control, and peer-to-peer transactions.

zkLogin on the Sui blockchain enables easy onboarding using familiar Web2 logins — no complicated seed phrases needed.

In short, it brings Amazon-like logistics to a decentralized, open-source world.

🧩 Real-World Use Cases Imagine a small hospital in rural India needing medical parts. Instead of importing them from another continent, a local 3D printer operator in the 3DOS network can download the blueprint and print it within hours.

In Africa, entrepreneurs are already using 3D printing to solve local challenges. 3DOS gives them a platform to monetize their efforts and collaborate globally.

And for designers, it’s a whole new marketplace — no middlemen, just pure innovation and reward.

💰 Print-to-Earn: A New Income Model At the heart of the 3DOS economy is “print-to-earn.” If you have a 3D printer, you can register it on the network, accept jobs, and earn in crypto. Think of it as mining — but instead of solving math problems, you're solving real-world needs.

This turns idle equipment into passive income machines — with real social impact.

Don’t have a printer? You can still participate by helping map data using the 3DOS browser extension — earning points and rewards for helping grow the network.

🔐 Privacy & Safety With growing concerns around surveillance and data abuse, 3DOS prioritizes privacy. Data collected via the extension is anonymized and encrypted. You contribute value to the network without giving up your personal information.

📈 Why Being Early Matters Web3 rewards early adopters — and 3DOS is still in its early stages. Participating now (whether through mining, data contribution, or content creation) gives you access to potential airdrops, NFT utilities, and governance rights in a global production network.

And let’s be real — few projects in the space are this practical, impactful, and ambitious.

Download the browser extension and start mapping data

If you have a 3D printer, register as a node

Join the community and start earning

🏁 Final Thoughts 3DOS isn’t just disrupting an industry — it’s creating a new one. One that is decentralized, inclusive, eco-friendly, and powered by people. Whether you're a techie, designer, or just a curious Web3 explorer, there’s a role for you in this ecosystem.

In the same way DeFi challenged traditional finance, 3DOS is challenging traditional manufacturing. And the best part? You can be a part of it.

items.title: AI Personal Finance Manager - Automated Wealth Building & Budget Optimization
items.author: ANIRUDDHA  ADAK
items.content:
A comprehensive personal finance management system that automatically tracks expenses, optimizes budgets, researches investments, monitors market conditions, and provides personalized financial advice with goal-based planning.

Personal finance management requires consistent attention and expertise most people lack. This system automates financial monitoring and provides professional-level insights for everyday investors.

Key Features Demonstrated:

  • Real-time expense tracking and categorization
  • Automated budget adjustments
  • Investment opportunity analysis
  • Risk assessment and recommendations

Personal Finance Agent Prompt: 

"Act as my comprehensive personal finance advisor and wealth management agent. Manage my complete financial picture with this framework:

FINANCIAL PROFILE:

Monthly income: [$8,500 after taxes]
Current savings: [$45,000 emergency fund, $120,000 investments]
Debt: [$280,000 mortgage at 3.2%, $0 credit card debt]
Goals: [Retire by 50, buy rental property in 2 years, children's education fund]
Risk tolerance: [Moderate to aggressive]
DAILY FINANCIAL MONITORING:

EXPENSE TRACKING:

Connect to all bank accounts and credit cards
Categorize and analyze all transactions automatically
Identify unusual spending patterns or potential fraud
Calculate daily/weekly spending against budget targets
BUDGET OPTIMIZATION:

Track spending across all categories (housing, food, entertainment, etc.)
Identify areas where I'm overspending vs. budget
Suggest specific cost-cutting opportunities
Reallocate budget surplus to savings/investment goals
INVESTMENT MONITORING:

Track portfolio performance across all accounts (401k, IRA, taxable)
Monitor individual stock/fund performance vs. benchmarks
Analyze asset allocation vs. target percentages
Identify rebalancing opportunities
MARKET RESEARCH:

Research potential investment opportunities (stocks, ETFs, REITs)
Monitor economic news affecting my portfolio
Track interest rate changes and their impact on my mortgage/savings
Identify tax optimization opportunities
WEEKLY STRATEGIC PLANNING:

Evaluate progress toward financial goals
Recommend specific actions to improve financial position
Research major purchases or financial decisions I'm considering
Calculate scenarios for different savings/investment strategies
Provide market outlook and portfolio adjustment recommendations
MONTHLY COMPREHENSIVE REVIEW:

Generate detailed financial health report
Update goal timelines based on current progress
Analyze tax implications of investment decisions
Research and recommend new financial products (high-yield savings, etc.)
Plan for upcoming major expenses or income changes
AUTOMATED ACTIONS:

Transfer excess budget surplus to high-yield savings
Rebalance investment portfolio when allocations drift >5%
Pay bills automatically while monitoring for errors
Set up automatic investments to maintain dollar-cost averaging
Send alerts for unusual transactions or account changes
Provide daily morning briefings and detailed weekly reports. Alert me immediately for time-sensitive opportunities or potential issues."
  1. Securely connect banking and investment accounts to Runner H
  2. Input your financial goals and risk tolerance
  3. Customize budget categories and limits
  4. Set up automatic transaction syncing
  5. Configure alert preferences
  6. Review and approve all automated actions initially
  7. Gradually increase automation as system proves reliable
  • Read-only access to financial accounts recommended
  • Enable two-factor authentication on all connections
  • Regular security audits of connected services
  • Never share full account credentials in prompts
  • Busy professionals who neglect personal finance management
  • Early-career individuals building wealth-building habits
  • Small business owners with irregular income
  • Anyone seeking to optimize their financial strategy
  • Increased savings rate by 15-25% through expense optimization
  • Improved investment returns through consistent monitoring and rebalancing
  • Reduced financial stress through automated oversight
  • Better goal achievement through systematic planning
  • Enhanced financial literacy through AI-generated insights
  • Emergency fund fully funded 6 months ahead of schedule
  • Investment portfolio outperforming benchmarks by 2-3%
  • Reduced monthly expenses by $400 while maintaining lifestyle
  • On track to meet early retirement goal

💰 My Runner H financial agent just helped me optimize my budget and found an extra $500/month for investments!

It's tracking every expense, monitoring my portfolio, and keeping me on track for early retirement. Personal finance automation is a game-changer!

items.title: How I Built a Self-Documenting GenAI-Native Remix Template
items.author: Anton
items.content:
This proof-of-concept bridges the gap between AI code generation and real-world engineering, all thanks to its underlying architecture. By embedding rules, tests, and docs directly into the scaffold, the system forces the AI to follow a strict two-phase workflow: (analyzing tasks, reading docs) and then (auto-linting, type-checking, and updating its own documentation). This turns the AI from an unpredictable assistant into a reliable partner that produces consistent, high-quality code.

What if your scaffold could enforce architecture **and* write its own docs?*

The internet is overflowing with “10× developer” prompt recipes, best‑practice checklists, and mega‑threads on how to squeeze perfect code out of LLMs.

Still, when I looked for something that solved my very real, day‑to‑day problems—keeping large‑scale projects consistent, maintainable, and well‑documented massive human overhead—I couldn’t find a solution that fit my needs.

In response, I built a Remix‑based starter project with AI‑guided scaffolding, architecture guardrails, and an example app generated entirely by the rules. The goal is to explore how we can make it painless to spin up and keep evolving—so quality stays high and standards stay intact.

If you’re curious how GenAI can help without turning your repo into spaghetti, read on to see the principles behind the template, the experiments that validated them, and the lessons you can apply to your own projects.

A quick note: This project is currently a working proof-of-concept, designed to explore the principles of "GenAI-Native" development. Think of it less as a production-ready tool and more as a research project and a source of ideas.

Motivation: The AI‑Hype vs. Real‑World Projects

Generative AI tools—GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, Cursor—promise warp‑speed dev, but teams still hit walls:

  • ❌ : AI ignores folder boundaries or sneaks in anti‑patterns.
  • ❌ : every codebase has its own sacred conventions.
  • ❌ : engineers still sift through PRs fixing style, tests, docs.

I set out to solve two challenges at once:

  1. Deliver production‑grade code , not just “sometimes good”.
  2. Keep architecture, docs, and tests up‑to‑date with .

Baking lint/architecture rules straight into a template felt like the quickest path to a predictable, AI‑augmented workflow.

The Core Concept: A High-Level View

At the heart of the template is a two-phase workflow designed to transform the AI from a chaotic code generator into a disciplined engineering partner. The first phase, acts as a mandatory analysis step. Before writing a single line of code, the AI is forced to behave like a senior developer: it must break down the task, ask clarifying questions, identify which files it will modify, and—most importantly—read the existing for any code it intends to touch. This initial, deliberate planning stage eradicates the common "rush-to-code" problem, ensuring that every action is contextual and well-considered.

Once the code is written, it immediately faces the second phase: a gauntlet of that serve as an unyielding quality gate. This isn't just a simple lint and type-check. It’s a comprehensive audit where the AI must automatically fix its own style errors, ensure perfect type-safety, and even perform a self-review for accessibility issues. This automated validation loop guarantees that no code reaches a human reviewer until it meets the project's rigorous standards for quality, consistency, and correctness.

The final, and perhaps most crucial, check in this process is the Mandatory Documentation Update. The system verifies that the AI has updated the file to reflect the changes it just made. This closes the loop, creating a self-documenting system where the code and its explanation are never out of sync. By combining proactive planning with rigorous, automated validation, this entire workflow ensures the AI doesn't just produce code, but contributes to a healthy, maintainable, and well-documented codebase.

It's worth noting that this level of consistency is achieved by being highly specific. While the overall pattern could be adapted for any stack, the current rules are intentionally hardcoded with Remix-specific (and other libs) conventions. This trade-off—sacrificing immediate flexibility for greater accuracy—ensures the AI has unambiguous context, leading to more predictable and reliable results.

From Chaos to Consistency

It took countless iterations of running, testing, and refining, but the rules eventually led to highly predictable behavior. The AI now consistently follows the architectural guardrails and uses the scaffolding scripts to generate new features, moving from unpredictable output to stable, structured results.

Rules forged through iteration

I ran ; when results disappointed me I tweaked the YAML and tried again. Over time the rule‑set converged into 18 guardrails that survive even edge‑cases. For example, at first, rules from were sometimes ignored or forgotten, so I had to introduce to ensure they were always enforced.

Single-prompt features & sustained autonomy

A medium-sized feature now completes end-to-end with a single prompt and runs continuously for 20–30 minutes without losing focus or quality, ultimately delivering fully working, well-structured code. For example prompt:

In this case, Claude Sonnet 4 via Cursor (without using Max mode) generated both the widget and the page in just 10 minutes. It automatically produced an auxiliary plan file task-plan-products-list.md, having assessed this as a complex, multi-step task (more than three steps). Below is a screenshot of the AI creating that plan file:

As a result, we ended up with a fully functional, well-written feature. Here are screenshots of the code snippet and the resulting page in action:

Yes, stricter rules cost time—but still beat manual work

Extra README edits, test stubs, and ESLint passes add overhead, yet the than hand‑coding. The bigger the codebase grows, the faster those upfront costs repay themselves through fewer bugs and easier maintenance.

Once a rule is green, adding more is easy—just remember to run a dedicated prompt that cross‑checks new rules against the existing set. Consistency + non‑contradiction are non‑negotiable for good results.

For example: you might start by creating a small set of base rules that define your project’s folder and file structure (e.g., “all feature slices must live under src/features/ and include a README.md, index.tsx, and styles.css”). Once those structural rules pass validation, you then introduce additional rules—say, conventions around naming components or importing shared utilities—and run a cross-validation prompt to ensure these new rules reference only the directories and files defined by your base rules, preventing any conflicts or ambiguities.

Example project in numbers

I spent roughly two days (around 8 hours each day, including breaks) working in a semi-manual loop of prompt → result → next task. Of that time, about 80 % was devoted to generating code, 15 % to validation and fixes, and the remaining 5 % to identifying and immediately applying enhancements to the rule set. The result is a featuring a type-safe API layer, clean FSD (Feature-Sliced Design) folders, passing tests, and up-to-date documentation—a solid proof that the ecosystem holds under real pressure.

The ruleset naturally evolved into more than a generator; it became an interactive specification. I could manually prototype a feature, then enter a review loop with the AI, instructing it to "refactor this file to match our conventions." This proved incredibly effective for ensuring consistency without sacrificing manual flexibility.

For example, if I accidentally create a component by hand in a components/ folder—which shouldn’t exist—I can ask the AI to review and fix it. The AI will detect the misplaced component and relocate it into the correct directory (e.g., under features/ or widgets/), updating imports and documentation as needed.

Tips on Generating Rules with AI

If you use an AI to help write your rules, I found a three-step process is essential for success:

  • : AI-written prompts are often verbose. By carefully editing them for conciseness, I was able to reduce the token count by an average of 61% across my rules without losing any semantic meaning.

    name: "Security Vulnerability Detection and Prevention"
description: |
  This rule implements comprehensive security scanning and validation
  to identify and prevent common web application vulnerabilities.
  It integrates with the FSD architecture methodology to ensure
  security considerations are properly addressed at each layer.

benefits:
  - "**Proactive Security**: Early detection of security vulnerabilities"
  - "**Comprehensive Coverage**: Scans for multiple vulnerability types"
  - "**Development Integration**: Seamless workflow integration"
  - "**Code Quality**: Maintains high security standards"
  - "**Risk Mitigation**: Reduces production security incidents"
  - "**Compliance Support**: Helps meet security requirements"

flagged_patterns:
  xss_vulnerabilities:
    description: "Cross-site scripting vulnerability patterns"
    examples:
      unescaped_html: |
        // ❌ BAD - Dangerous innerHTML usage
        <div dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{__html: userInput}} />

        // ❌ BAD - Direct DOM manipulation with user data
        element.innerHTML = userData;

    name: "Security Vulnerability Detection"
description: "Detects common web vulnerabilities in FSD architecture"

flagged_patterns:
  xss_vulnerabilities:
    - "dangerouslySetInnerHTML with user input"
    - "innerHTML with unescaped data"
    - "document.write() usage"

  injection_vulnerabilities:
    - "eval() with dynamic content"
    - "SQL queries with string concatenation"
    - "Unsafe regex patterns"

  • : This is critical. You must check new rules against existing ones to ensure they don't create contradictions. A conflicting rule-set is a recipe for chaotic output.

  • : A rule that looks perfect on paper can fail in execution. Always run it through a real-world scenario to see the actual output.

The core idea isn’t a particular stack—it’s a mesh of principles and mutually reinforcing rules. Remix + Tailwind are tactical choices, but the pattern is supposed to work with any modern framework.

For this proof-of-concept, I chose a specific set of technologies to test the principles in a real-world scenario. Each choice was made to maximize the chances of getting clean, predictable output from the AI:

Remix provides first-class routing and a data-layer out of the box, SSR when you need it, and a compiler-less mindset that keeps bundle sizes sane. The strict type-safety from TypeScript is not a luxury, but a fundamental requirement for maintaining quality with AI-generated code.
The atomic nature of Tailwind is a perfect match for GenAI. An LLM can easily compose complex interfaces from small, understandable building blocks, which means almost no time is wasted on routine styling. Using shadcn/ui on top provides accessible primitives, ensuring the generated UI is high-quality from the start.
Feature‑Sliced Design (FSD)Clear vertical slices (, , ) enforce SRP, scale cleanly for big teams, and pair perfectly with DI (Dependency injection) rules—AI can’t “accidentally” reach across layers.
Rules live in neutral YAML. A conversion script then translates them into platform-specific formats. For instance, they can be converted into rules for Cursor, or prompts for Claude, Copilot, and Windsurf.
Plop.js offers deterministic scaffolding based on templates. This is crucial as it creates a predictable file structure every time, forcing the AI to only fill in specific logic instead of generating boilerplate. This approach guarantees architectural consistency and significantly reduces token costs.
Any state/query lib would work; RTKQ ships cache, tagging, polling, and optimistic updates out of the box, so AI focuses on business logic, not plumbing.
Jest + Playwright + jest‑axe. The concrete libs are less important than the : AI must output tests, period.
Acts as the final quality gate. ESLint enforces not only standard code style but also custom architectural rules, such as ensuring Feature-Sliced Design (FSD) boundaries are respected. TypeScript provides strict type-safety, an industry-standard practice that is absolutely essential for validating AI-generated code.

The template assumes that LLM prompts do almost all the mechanical work while humans supervise and merge. Four core practices make that possible:

Every new feature or component is generated through a Plop command backed by Handlebars templates. The model never creates folders by hand—it only fills in logic. This keeps token usage low, generation fast, and the file tree perpetually aligned with the architecture.

Each slice of the codebase owns a . The upper half is written for humans; the lower half contains meta-instructions for the AI. Before making any code changes, the model read that README, and after a change, it update it. I specifically asked Claude Sonnet 4 to evaluate the usefulness of this README approach, and it rated the pattern for improving accuracy and reducing hallucinations.

Hands‑free lint & type loop

After finishing a task, the model triggers and . If either fails, the same session patches the errors before a human even opens the PR. Result: style, a11y, and type issues rarely reach code review.

Rule‑driven orchestration

All of the above are wired together through platform‑agnostic YAML rules: scaffold → write code → lint → doc update. Each new prompt inherits a clean, validated context, so the codebase tends to improve with time instead of degrading.

In effect, the rules the LLM into producing tidy, decomposed code, and that tidy code then makes every AI session easier. The system becomes a feedback loop that keeps large projects from collapsing under their own weight.

While this GenAI-native Remix template already delivers reliable, high-quality results, it remains a work in progress. Further tuning of rule granularity, performance optimizations in the orchestration scripts, and expanded coverage for edge-case scenarios will be essential to unlock its full potential. I look forward to refining these guardrails and sharing future improvements as the ecosystem continues to evolve.

Use to clone the repository into a new folder without the Git history.

npx degit tohachan/remix-genai-template my-genai-app

Navigate into your new project folder and install the necessary packages.

cd my-genai-app
npm install

Run the init script. This will convert the agnostic YAML rules into a specific format for your chosen AI development environment.

4. Select Your Environment & Go! You will be prompted in the terminal to choose your target environment (e.g., Cursor, Claude, etc.). Once the script finishes, simply open the project folder in your selected tool (for example, Open Folder in Cursor), and you're ready to start building with AI guardrails!

Questions? Ideas? Drop a comment or open an issue—would love to see what you build! 😄

items.title: Machine Learning Fundamentals: anomaly detection tutorial
items.author: DevOps Fundamental
items.content:
In Q3 2023, a critical regression in our fraud detection model resulted in a 17% increase in false positives, triggering a cascade of customer service escalations and a temporary halt to new account creation. Root cause analysis revealed a subtle drift in feature distribution – specifically, a change in the average transaction amount for a newly onboarded demographic.  Existing model monitoring focused on overall accuracy, failing to detect this nuanced shift. This incident underscored the necessity of robust anomaly detection  the ML system lifecycle, not merely as a post-deployment check.  

Anomaly detection, in this context, isn’t simply about identifying outliers in data. It’s a critical component of a comprehensive MLOps strategy, spanning data ingestion validation, model training quality control, real-time inference monitoring, and even model deprecation signaling.  It directly addresses compliance requirements (e.g., model fairness, explainability), supports scalable inference demands (by identifying performance regressions), and is fundamental to maintaining service level objectives (SLOs) for ML-powered applications.



From a systems perspective, “anomaly detection” in production ML isn’t a single algorithm, but a distributed system of checks and balances. It’s the orchestration of multiple detectors operating across the entire ML pipeline.  These detectors analyze data quality, model performance, feature distributions, and system metrics. 

It interacts heavily with:
 For tracking model versions, parameters, and metrics, enabling comparison of baseline performance against current deployments.
 Orchestrating data validation, feature engineering, and model retraining pipelines, with anomaly detection integrated as data quality checks.
  Distributing anomaly detection computations across large datasets during training and batch inference.
  Deploying and scaling anomaly detection services alongside inference endpoints.
 Monitoring feature drift and data quality at the source, triggering alerts when anomalies are detected.
 Leveraging built-in monitoring tools and integrating custom anomaly detection logic.

Key trade-offs involve the balance between sensitivity (detecting all anomalies) and specificity (minimizing false positives). System boundaries must clearly define which anomalies trigger alerts, automated rollbacks, or manual intervention. Common implementation patterns include statistical process control (SPC) charts, autoencoders for reconstruction error analysis, and isolation forests for outlier detection.

 Detecting statistically significant deviations in key metrics during A/B tests, indicating potential bugs or unintended consequences of new model versions. (E-commerce)
  Identifying performance regressions or unexpected behavior immediately after deploying a new model version, triggering automated rollback to the previous stable version. (Fintech)
  Detecting violations of fairness constraints or regulatory requirements by monitoring model predictions and feature distributions for bias. (Health Tech)
  Validating the quality of labels generated by human annotators or automated labeling systems, identifying inconsistencies or errors that could degrade model performance. (Autonomous Systems)
 Detecting anomalies in inference latency, throughput, or resource utilization, indicating potential infrastructure bottlenecks or failures. (All verticals)

mermaid A[Data Source] --> B(Data Ingestion);

B --> C{Data Validation & Anomaly Detection (Airflow)};

C -- Data Quality Issues --> D[Alerting & Data Quarantine];

C -- Clean Data --> E(Feature Store);

E --> F(Model Training);

F --> G(MLflow - Model Registry);

G --> H(Model Deployment - Kubernetes);

H --> I(Inference Endpoint);

I --> J{Inference Monitoring & Anomaly Detection (Prometheus/Evidently)};

J -- Performance Degradation --> K[Automated Rollback/Alerting];

J -- Data Drift --> L[Retraining Pipeline Trigger]; I --> M(Feedback Loop);


Typical workflow: Data is ingested, validated (including anomaly detection on raw data), and stored in a feature store. Models are trained, registered in MLflow, and deployed to Kubernetes. Inference requests are routed to the endpoint, and real-time monitoring (Prometheus, Evidently) detects anomalies in predictions, latency, or feature distributions.  Traffic shaping (Istio) and canary rollouts are used to minimize the impact of potential regressions. Rollback mechanisms are triggered automatically based on predefined thresholds.

## 5. Implementation Strategies

**Python Orchestration (Data Validation):**

python import numpy as np

def detect_data_drift(df, column, threshold=3): """Detects data drift using Z-score.""" std = df[column].std()

z_scores = np.abs((df[column] - mean) / std)

drift_count = np.sum(z_scores > threshold)

return drift_count > len(df) * 0.05 # 5% drift threshold

data = pd.read_csv("transaction_data.csv") if detect_data_drift(data, "transaction_amount"):

raise ValueError("Data drift detected in transaction_amount!")


**Kubernetes Deployment (Canary):**

yaml kind: Deployment name: fraud-detection-v2 replicas: 1 # Canary replica

selector: matchLabels: version: v2 metadata: app: fraud-detection spec: - name: fraud-detection # ... other container settings


**Bash Script (Experiment Tracking):**

bash

mlflow experiments create -n "FraudDetectionExperiment"

mlflow runs create -e "FraudDetectionExperiment" -r "v1.0"

mlflow log_param "learning_rate" 0.01

mlflow log_metric "accuracy" 0.95


## 6. Failure Modes & Risk Management

* **Stale Models:**  Models not updated frequently enough to adapt to changing data patterns. *Mitigation:* Automated retraining pipelines triggered by data drift or performance degradation.
* **Feature Skew:**  Differences in feature distributions between training and inference data. *Mitigation:*  Continuous monitoring of feature distributions in production, data validation checks.
* **Latency Spikes:**  Increased inference latency due to infrastructure bottlenecks or model complexity. *Mitigation:* Autoscaling, caching, model optimization, circuit breakers.
* **False Positives:**  Incorrectly identifying normal behavior as anomalous. *Mitigation:*  Adjusting anomaly detection thresholds, incorporating contextual information.
* **Data Poisoning:** Malicious data injected into the training pipeline. *Mitigation:* Robust data validation, access control, and audit logging.

## 7. Performance Tuning & System Optimization

Metrics: P90/P95 latency, throughput (requests/second), model accuracy, infrastructure cost.

Techniques:

* **Batching:** Processing multiple inference requests in a single batch to improve throughput.
* **Caching:** Caching frequently accessed features or model predictions.
* **Vectorization:** Utilizing vectorized operations for faster computation.
* **Autoscaling:** Dynamically adjusting the number of replicas based on traffic load.
* **Profiling:** Identifying performance bottlenecks in the model or infrastructure.

Anomaly detection itself adds overhead. Optimizing anomaly detection algorithms and minimizing the frequency of checks are crucial.

## 8. Monitoring, Observability & Debugging

* **Prometheus:**  Collecting system metrics (CPU, memory, latency).
* **Grafana:**  Visualizing metrics and creating dashboards.
* **OpenTelemetry:**  Tracing requests across distributed systems.
* **Evidently:**  Monitoring data drift and model performance.
* **Datadog:** Comprehensive observability platform.

Critical Metrics:  Anomaly score, data drift metrics, inference latency, throughput, error rate, resource utilization. Alert conditions should be defined based on predefined thresholds. Log traces should include relevant context for debugging.

## 9. Security, Policy & Compliance

* **Audit Logging:**  Tracking all model deployments, data access, and anomaly detection events.
* **Reproducibility:**  Ensuring that models can be reliably reproduced from source code and data.
* **Secure Model/Data Access:**  Implementing strict access control policies to protect sensitive data and models.
* **Governance Tools:**  OPA (Open Policy Agent) for enforcing policies, IAM (Identity and Access Management) for controlling access, Vault for managing secrets, ML metadata tracking for lineage.

## 10. CI/CD & Workflow Integration

Using Argo Workflows:

yaml

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1

kind: Workflow generateName: anomaly-detection-pipeline- entrypoint: anomaly-detection

  • name: anomaly-detection steps:
    • - name: data-validation template: data-validation-step
    • - name: model-evaluation template: model-evaluation-step dependencies: [data-validation] 

Deployment gates and automated tests should be integrated into the CI/CD pipeline to prevent regressions. Rollback logic should be automated based on predefined thresholds.

## 11. Common Engineering Pitfalls

* **Ignoring Data Quality:**  Failing to validate data before training or inference.
* **Overly Sensitive Anomaly Detection:**  Generating too many false positives.
* **Lack of Contextual Information:**  Treating all anomalies equally, without considering the specific context.
* **Insufficient Monitoring:**  Not tracking the right metrics or not having adequate alerting.
* **Ignoring Feature Drift:**  Failing to detect and address changes in feature distributions.

## 12. Best Practices at Scale

Mature ML platforms (Michelangelo, Cortex) emphasize:

* **Centralized Monitoring:**  A single pane of glass for monitoring all ML systems.
* **Automated Retraining:**  Continuous retraining pipelines triggered by data drift or performance degradation.
* **Feature Store Integration:**  Leveraging a feature store for consistent feature definitions and data quality monitoring.
* **Scalability Patterns:**  Horizontal scaling, distributed processing, and caching.
* **Operational Cost Tracking:**  Monitoring the cost of running ML systems and identifying opportunities for optimization.

## 13. Conclusion

Anomaly detection is not a “nice-to-have” but a “must-have” for production ML systems. It’s a foundational component of a robust MLOps strategy, enabling reliable, scalable, and compliant ML-powered applications.  Next steps include benchmarking different anomaly detection algorithms, integrating anomaly detection into existing CI/CD pipelines, and conducting regular audits to ensure the effectiveness of anomaly detection systems.  Continuous improvement and adaptation are key to maintaining the health and performance of large-scale ML operations.

items.title: AI image models are getting INSANELY good, this might change LLMs forever, OpenAI Deep Research API, Google Gemma 3n, and more
items.author: This Week in AI Engineering
items.content:
Welcome to the Twenty-Fifth edition of "This Week in AI Engineering"!

This week, OpenAI expands its API with new Deep Research and Webhooks modules, Google released Gemma 3n for multimodal use on low-resource devices, and Gemini CLI hits the terminal. Meanwhile, Sakana.ai unveiled a new framework for reasoning via reinforcement-based teacher models, Higgsfield dropped a stunning new aesthetic model called Soul, and FLUX.1 Kontext dev released an image editor that rivals proprietary tools.

As always, we’ll wrap things up with under-the-radar tools and releases that deserve your attention.

Higgsfield Soul: The Most Aesthetic AI Photo Model

is the newest photo-only model by Higgsfield.ai, and it’s trained specifically to hit magazine-level visual quality out of the box.

  • on internal AestheticNet benchmarks for texture, lighting, and color fidelity.

  • : 50+ fashion‑grade styles, from “Quiet Luxury” to “Y2K Retro”

  • : Unlike generalist diffusion models, Soul is laser‑tuned for still imagery.

  • : Retains facial features and fine details across diverse poses and lighting.

  • : One‑click application of editorial looks.\ : Adjust contrast, grain, color saturation, and mood.

  • : Rapid generation of campaign stills with consistent branding.

  • : On‑demand professional headshots and social media avatars.

  • : Product photography with consistent studio‑grade lighting.

FLUX.1 Kontext [dev]: Open Weights, Proprietary-Level Image Editing

, developed under FLUX.1, is now available as an that delivers image editing capabilities comparable to top proprietary tools.

Model Specs & Open Weights

  • : Optimized for local & global edits.

  • Open Non‑Commercial License: Weights on Hugging Face with support for ComfyUI, Diffusers, and TensorRT.

  • Iterative In‑Context Edits: Modify images step‑by‑step without drift.

  • : Maintains subject identity across multiple edits.

  • : Text + image prompts for precise control.

  • : Outperforms open models (e.g., Bagel, HiDream‑E1) and closed systems (Gemini‑Flash Image) on human preference tests.

  • : BF16, FP8, FP4 TensorRT options for speed–quality trade‑offs.

  • : Fully open‑source, research‑focused.

  • : Commercial tiers offering faster renders (3–5 s), advanced typography, and enterprise SLAs.

  • : Embed studio‑grade editing into web and desktop apps.

  • : Designers can test visual concepts on consumer hardware.

  • : Study flow matching and iterative editing without license barriers.

For developers building creative tooling, Kontext provides a transparent, tunable base model with no license constraints. Think of it as a Photoshop-grade layer under your AI product, completely open.

This Might Change LLMs Forever

Learning‑to‑Teach Framework

  • Prompted with Question + Answer: RLTs receive both the problem and its solution, focusing on crafting clear, step‑by‑step explanations.

  • : Teachers are rewarded based on how well a student LLM internalizes the lesson, measured via student log‑probabilities.

  • : Continuous feedback from student performance enables efficient RL on 7 B parameter teacher models.

  • Distillation‑Ready Outputs: Explanations directly serve as training data for downstream student models.

  • : RLTs distilled into students that outperform pipelines using orders‑of‑magnitude larger LMs.

  • : Maintains reasoning efficacy on out‑of‑distribution benchmarks without additional tuning.

  • : Build high‑performance reasoning assistants without massive compute or retraining costs.

  • : Automate generation of teaching materials for specialized domains.

  • : Rapidly adapt student models for new tasks by swapping in different RLT teachers.

It’s still early research, but this could be a breakthrough for cheaper, more scalable logic-intensive systems.

OpenAI API Adds Deep Research & Webhooks

OpenAI just added two powerful capabilities to its developer API, and , unlocking a whole new layer of intelligence and interactivity for agent-based apps.

  • o3‑deep‑research & o4‑mini‑deep‑research: These models synthesize across hundreds of web sources, returning structured, cited reports instead of snippets.

  • Autonomous Multistep Reasoning: Agents can now initiate deep dives on complex topics, market research, technical reviews, academic surveys, directly from code.

  • : $10 per 1M input tokens, $40 per 1M output tokens.

  • : $2 per 1M input tokens, $8 per 1M output tokens.

  • : Designed for background execution, pairing Deep Research with Webhooks to avoid timeouts and network issues.

  • : Receive callbacks when long‑running tasks (e.g., deep research jobs) complete, eliminating the need for polling.

  • : Supports authenticated endpoints and structured payloads, ideal for batch processing, CI/CD pipelines, or CRM triggers.

  • Automated Competitive Analysis: Agents that track and report on new

  • : Build workflows that automatically generate literature reviews or technical audits.

  • : Tie into ticketing systems or dashboards for on‑demand deep dives.

Together, these tools shift OpenAI’s API toward dynamic, live agent ecosystems, not just static prompting.

Google Releases Gemma 3n: Light, Open, Multimodal

Google has officially dropped , the newest entry in its lightweight open model family, built on the same core research as Gemini.

  • MatFormer Backbone & PLE Caching: Parameter‑efficient layers and per‑layer embedding caches reduce compute and memory footprint.

  • E2B & E4B Variants: Available in 2 B and 4 B parameter sizes, optimized for different performance–efficiency trade‑offs.

Multimodal & Multilingual

  • Native support for text, images, video, and audio.

  • Language Coverage: Pretrained on 140+ spoken languages for text; 35 languages for multimodal tasks.

Efficiency & On‑Device Performance

  • Runs entirely on-device, ideal for privacy‑sensitive or low‑connectivity scenarios.

  • 2 GB RAM Footprint: Enables AI on smartphones, tablets, and edge hardware without cloud dependency.

  • Local chatbots that understand voice, image, and text queries.

  • Privacy‑First Apps: Healthcare or finance tools where data never leaves the device.

  • Field Research: Offline translation and multimodal analysis for remote areas.

Whether you're building local AI assistants, mobile multimodal apps, or multilingual chat interfaces, Gemma 3n is a powerful, open alternative to proprietary multimodal giants.

Gemini CLI Brings AI to the Terminal

Google also quietly launched , an open-source command-line interface that puts Gemini directly into your dev terminal.

  • Natural‑Language Prompts: Code generation, bug fixes, documentation, research queries.

  • MCP & Real‑Time Data: Leverages Google’s Model Context Protocol to fetch live web data when needed.

  • Multimodal Extensions: Integrations with Imagen and Veo for image/video generation.

  • and 1,000 requests/day free (via Gemini Code Assist license).

  • 1 M token context window for complex, multi‑step prompts.

Developer Experience & Extensibility

  • Explore code, contribute plugins, extend functionality.

  • ReAct Loop: Reason‑and‑act framework to chain local tools, scripts, and cloud services.

  • Terminal‑First Workflows: Reduce context‑switching for devs who prefer shells.

  • CI/CD Automation: Scripted AI checks for code quality or task orchestration.

  • Ad‑hoc Research: Quick content generation and data lookup without leaving the terminal.

For engineers tired of context-switching to chat UIs, Gemini CLI is a productivity boost you can script.

Tools & Releases YOU Should Know About

is an agentic development environment designed to accelerate software creation using AI. It enables you to spawn and orchestrate multiple agents in parallel, each handling specific tasks in a development workflow. From writing boilerplate code to debugging and documentation, Warp 2.0 abstracts complex development processes into coordinated agent actions, making it ideal for high-velocity engineering teams looking to boost productivity through AI-native workflows.

is an AI developer assistant that supports your daily programming needs—whether it's writing algorithms, debugging runtime errors, testing code, or answering technical questions. Gru.ai acts like a tireless pair programmer, helping you move faster through coding tasks by offering intelligent, context-aware suggestions across a wide range of languages and frameworks. It’s a valuable tool for solo developers and teams looking to reduce friction in the coding lifecycle.

is a full-stack AI development agent that lets you build, test, and deploy complete applications with minimal effort. It integrates seamlessly with Supabase for backend functionality and offers one-click deployment via Vercel, removing the need for manual setup. Whether you're prototyping or building production-ready apps, GoCodeo compresses hours of engineering work into minutes with its intuitive agent-driven automation.

enhances code comprehension and team collaboration through AI-powered, context-sensitive documentation. By leveraging static analysis and machine-generated explanations, Swimm integrates directly into IDEs like VSCode, JetBrains, IntelliJ, and PyCharm. It helps developers navigate unfamiliar codebases by providing inline documentation that evolves with your code—minimizing onboarding time and reducing the cognitive load of maintaining technical knowledge across teams.

And that wraps up this issue of "This Week in AI Engineering", brought to you by — your flight recorder for AI apps! Non-deterministic AI issues are hard to repro, unless you have Jam! Instant replay the session, prompt + logs to debug ⚡️

Thank you for tuning in! Be sure to share this newsletter with your fellow AI enthusiasts and follow for more weekly updates.

Until next time, happy building!

items.title: Mirai Test - Test The Future
items.author: Stress Out
items.content:
I built a tool that lets you write test cases in plain English, and it generates + runs them automatically using ChatGPT and Playwright.

Just launched version 1.1.4 — includes a Test Generator UI, smarter AI prediction, and faster element scanning.

As someone who’s worked in testing and automation for years, I’ve always found this contradiction:

  • Testers in natural language
  • But automated tests need
  • And most UI tests break way too often from small DOM changes

I wanted a way to bridge that gap — without requiring testers to become coders or maintain brittle scripts.

So I started hacking on something I needed for myself. That became .

MiraiTest is an AI-powered web testing framework.

You write a simple prompt like:

Login to example.com Verify the logout button is visible

  1. Use to generate Playwright test code
  2. Save logs and stable test cases
  3. Output results in a clean HTML report

🆕 What’s new in version 1.1.4

  • 🧪 (CLI is still there if you prefer)
  • ⚡ – better performance on large apps
  • 🧠 – better handling of vague/short prompts
  • 📄 Debug logs and test case storage included by default
  • Wrapper: Custom CLI (Node.js) + file manager
  • Output: HTML reporter + JSON logs

💬 What does “Mirai” mean?

I forgot to explain this in my video, but: means in Japanese.

And honestly — I want testing to feel like it belongs there.

  • [ ] Open-source the CLI tool
  • [ ] Build a lightweight desktop UI (Electron?)
  • [ ] Add GitHub Actions integration

- [ ] Fine-tune LLM prompts via few-shot examples

  • Feedback on the UX / prompt structure
  • Suggestions for devtools / QA integration
  • Connecting with others building in AI + automation

Thanks for reading 🙏

You can follow the journey here or on

LinkedIn.

P.S. If you’re curious about testing with LLMs, I’d love to talk.

items.title: 20 Best AI Headshot Generators of 2025 (I Tried Them All)
items.author: Artturi Jalli
items.content:
I spent $3,100 on AI headshots to figure out which one is the best.

In this post, I will show you my results and show you the best AI headshot generators on the market.

This list has tools for free headshots, paid headshots, customizable headshots, and more.

Here’s a short summary of the top 3 AI headshot generators right now:

Disclaimer: This post is supported by readers. It has some affiliate links at no cost to you. Thanks for the support!

Let’s move on to the results and more tools!

My Results: Quick Overview

This part is for the busy readers. I'll show you the top headshot generators with my favorite headshots.

BetterPic is my top pick for an AI headshot generator. It doesn’t just make amazing photos that really look like you, but it also lets you tweak them however you like. Plus, they have skilled human editors who add the final polish to your pictures.

HeadshotsByAI is another AI headshot generator I really like. The photos come out bright, clear, and very lifelike. The only downside is that you can’t make any edits to the pictures.

Secta AI is an excellent AI headshot generator that lets you create professional shots as well as more relaxed, casual photos. It also offers some of the top editing features out there. Plus, the Northern Lights style headshots are just stunning!

HeadshotPro is a solid and flexible AI headshot generator. It’s the only one here that also makes headshots for public speaking. Like the rest, it’s simple to use, and the photos turn out looking fantastic!

TryItOn makes great-looking headshots for both social media and professional use. It offers plenty of ways to customize and edit your photos. You can even turn one of your headshots into a video if you’d like.

InstaHeadshots is the top AI tool for making social media profile pictures. You can’t tweak the images, but the results are impressively good. Most of the time, I got selfies that were perfect for my social media accounts.

HeadShot Kiwi isn’t the most well-known headshot generator, but it’s definitely a solid choice. It makes nice, realistic photos that look more natural and less awkward than what you get from many other tools out there.

ProPhotos is one of the top options for professional headshots. If you want to look sharp in a suit and get clear, realistic photos without spending a lot, this tool is a great pick. Just keep in mind it doesn’t offer editing or customization features.

Photopacks is a nice, budget-friendly headshot generator. It’s the most affordable option that still delivers quality photos. You won’t be able to edit the images, but you do get plenty of great headshots to choose from.

Another budget-friendly option on this list is AI SuitUp. It makes headshots that look a lot like those from the top tools but for less money. Just know that you can’t edit the photos with this one either.

The Multiverse AI is one of the earliest AI headshot generators around. It’s not only one of the first, but it also lets you customize your photos nicely. All you have to do is describe the tool and what you’d like in your picture, and it will create it for you. The only drawback is that you can’t edit your images afterward.

Aragon AI is one of the “OGs” of AI headshots, launched back in 2022. It helps you make realistic headshots that are great for professional use. It also comes with solid editing and customization options to fine-tune your photos.

If you’re after vintage-style profile photos, I’d suggest giving PortraitPal a try. The results might not be the very best, but the headshots have a calm, pleasant feel to them.

Fiverr is a freelancer marketplace, and as you’d expect, you can find AI headshot services there as well. It’s not really a tool, but more like hiring a professional editor who uses their own AI tech to make your headshots.

In early 2025, ChatGPT updated its image generation technology, so now you can make AI headshots right here. All you need to do is upload a photo of yourself in the chat and ask it to turn it into a professional headshot.

ProfileBakery isn’t the best AI headshot generator, but it’s not the worst either. It creates slightly artificial, 3D-style headshots that you can still use for professional purposes.

Then there are the free AI headshot generators. With these, the quality goes down a lot because they rely on cheaper, free technology to make the images. Out of all the ones I tried, HeadshotMaster.io was the best free option.

AIEase is probably the fastest headshot generator here. You don’t have to pay or even create an account. But because it’s free, the quality of the photos isn’t the greatest.

Then there are AI headshot tools that use background-swap technology. Instead of recreating your face, they only replace the background. HeadshotPro, for example, offers a free background changer with plenty of options you can pick from.

PFPMaker is another AI headshot tool that simply swaps out your background. It gives you plenty of backgrounds to pick and lets you customize your photos in many ways.

ChatGPT said: Finally, last but not least, there’s Canva.

It’s not the top headshot maker, but it’s worth mentioning because it has a lot of handy features, and many people already use it.

These headshots work well for quick drafts or brainstorming if you’d rather stay inside the app.

Plus, the technology is improving, and there are already plenty of AI headshot apps in Canva that can help you get even better results.

Here’s a quick table that summarizes the prices of these AI headshot generators:

$35 (Basic), $39 (Pro), $79 (Expert)Best overall, human edits, team-ready
$29 (Basic), $39 (Standard), $59 (Premium)Sharp lighting, no editing
$49 one-time + $5–$20/mo for editsAurora-style, HEIC support, ~140 results
$29 (Basic), $39 (Professional), $59 (Executive)Public-speaking styles, background edits
$35 (20), $49 (50), $61 (100) + $9/human editFastest, versatile sizes, some editing
$49 (Starter), $59 (Basic), $69 (Premium)Best for social media, fast, great selfies
$29 (Starter), $39 (Professional), $59 (Executive)
$25 (Basic), $55 (Premium), $155 (Professional)Professional look, no editing
Cheapest decent results, no edits
$27 (Basic), $37 (Professional), $57 (Ultimate)Good for LinkedIn, no customizations
$35 (Starter), $45 (Basic), $75 (Premium)Good editing, overfitted poses
Free (3–5/day), Paid plan for moreUltra-custom, generic look
$35 (Starter), $45 (Basic), $75 (Premium)Vintage vibe, simple to use
$24 (Mini), $29 (Regular), $49 (Pro)3D-style results, shared folders
No signup, fastest free option

But now, let's move on to the actual list with detailed reviews, results, final verdicts, and more. Hope you like it!

BetterPic is my all-time favorite AI headshot generator.

It gave me the best results overall and offered the most ways to customize my photos.

This tool has fantastic customization options, and the highlight is the human editing feature. A real editor goes over your pictures to make sure they really look like you (you get this with the Expert plan).

Let me show you some of the photos I created with it.

Before I show you the results, let me show you how to use it.

To start creating your headshots with AI, sign up here and read the rules. I got myself an expert plan to use the human edits feature later.

Then answer the basic questions about your characteristics:

Pick some styles for your photoshoots:

For the best results, I recommend choosing the three available styles.

Read the image instructions carefully.

This is the most important step because here you teach the AI what you look like.

You’re basically training the AI headshot generator to learn your features so it can place you in different settings later on.

I suggest using photos with different lighting, clothes, and poses to help the AI get the most accurate idea of your appearance.

Here are the images that I sent to the AI:

As you can see, those are just regular photos of me from my camera roll that I’ve taken over the years.

After I picked the images, the tool checked their quality and removed any with low resolution. Honestly, this part is the “hardest” because it can take some time to find enough good photos for the AI headshot generator.

But once I had my images ready, it was finally time to create my AI headshots.

It took roughly 30 minutes for the AI to create my headshots.

That always makes me smile, even though I’ve looked at my AI headshots many times by now.

They’re just so good—really accurate, realistic, and crisp.

I’m actually using one of these photos on my LinkedIn profile too.

Here are some more close-up headshots that I got from BetterPic:

I won’t say that every single image turned out perfect.

Like BetterPic explains on the results page, most of the photos look great, some are just decent, and a few don’t come out quite right.

For example, in this one, you can see small artifacts on my face and teeth. They’re not huge, but you can notice them.

The nice thing about BetterPic is that you can fix those little AI flaws quickly and easily.

All you have to do is click the BP AI Edit button.

You can also ask the BetterPic team for a manual edit so they can make the photo look exactly like you.

That’s really helpful, especially if your team wants all the images to have a consistent look.

For example, here’s an AI headshot I got from BetterPic:

It looks good, but I’d like to switch up my outfit… Luckily, that’s easy to do with BetterPic thanks to its customization tools.

To change the clothes in one of your AI headshots, just pick the image and click “BP AI Edit.”

Pick the “Clothing” option from the AI edits. Here, I've picked a Christmas outfit:

Here are the results I got back after about 5 minutes of waiting:

Isn’t that cool? It’s the same picture as before, but now it has a Christmas theme.

I thought it was so fun that I decided to try a few more styles. So I asked the AI to put me in a dark suit and also dress me up like a doctor.

Here’s what it came up with:

What an incredible piece of AI this is.

The other customization options are really nice, too.

For example, you can expand your AI headshots.

Take this headshot—it looks great overall, but the problem is it feels a little too zoomed in for my taste.

To fix that, I just used the AI expand feature in the editing tools.

When I did this, the AI headshot generator added extra space to the top of the photo.

Honestly, this is the best AI headshot tool out there. I really like how versatile it is and how much freedom you have to customize your photos.

You can also create headshots for your whole team.

If you click the top left corner, you’ll see your account, and right below that, there’s a Team option.

The team feature is fantastic. There’s an Admin view where you can invite your team members to make their headshots.

You can pick a shared style for everyone or let each person choose their own. Once your team has created their headshots, all the images show up in the Admin view for you to see.

This is really helpful because you don’t have to collect headshots one by one from everyone.

You can find a guide on how to use the BetterPic team feature here.

I think the biggest issue with many other headshot generators is that the results are just a 6/10 or 7/10, and you can’t edit or fix them. But with BetterPic, that’s not a problem because you can sort out most issues with the AI editing tools or the Human Edits feature.

  • Faster than most AI headshot generators

  • Very realistic outputs with a lot of versatility

  • AI customization options (background removal, eye color, attire changes) with human edits available

  • Picking images might take time. Also, the turnaround is about 30-60 minutes. That's not bad compared with real photoshoots, but it's still not instantaneous.

  • Sometimes the images have flaws (odd eyes, imperfect teeth), but this is similar. to all the other AI headshot generators.

      I love BetterPic. The results look realistic, and it’s also quicker than most other AI headshot generators.

      You get a lot of ways to customize and edit your photos, even with help from a real human editor on their team.

      This is the best. It’s the standard I’ll use to compare all the other tools on this list.

      Just keep in mind that your own features can affect the results. Even with the top AI headshot generators, I’ve heard of people having issues. For example, one person said the AI kept giving them short hair even though they actually have long hair, which isn’t ideal.

      Next, let me introduce HeadshotsByAI. This is another AI headshot generator that takes your photos from your camera roll and turns them into professional-looking images.

      The results are very sharp and clear, almost as good as BetterPic. Honestly, I’d probably rank this tool at the very top if it weren’t for one thing—it doesn’t let you edit your AI headshots. You can only view and download them.

      Let me show you some of the photos I got.

      Once again, let me start by showing you how to use this tool if you’d like to try it out yourself. After that, I’ll share the results I got.

      First, go to the HeadshotsByAI homepage and sign up.

      Pick the “Personal Branding” option to begin creating photos of yourself.

      Then, from the dashboard, select your gallery:

      Add your details, such as gender, eye color, and more.

      Then, pick the input images of yourself. Here are the ones I used:

      Then hit “Start training”. This sends your photos to the “AI photographer” to train with.

      It takes a second for the AI to create your headshots. I'd say it took about 60 minutes for me.

      The tool lets you know once the images are ready with an email.

      These look so realistic you’d never know they were made by AI.

      Here are a few more photos the AI created for me:

      I really love the lighting and the outfits in these photos.

      The quality and lighting here are better than any other tool I’ve tried.

      That said, in some shots, my forehead looks a bit too tall—but that’s probably just me overanalyzing after years of staring at myself in the mirror.

      There are also a few little flaws, like some messed-up teeth and skin that looks too smooth:

      • Quicker than most AI headshots

      • Very realistic headshots with only 8 input photos

      • User-friendly interface that takes no time to learn how to use

      • 60 minutes of waiting is slower than the fastest AI headshots

      • No editing options out there!

      • Some images look uncanny and not too realistic

      • : $29 for 30 headshots

      • : $39 for 60 headshots

      • : $59 for 120 headshots

        These are definitely some of the best headshots out there. I couldn’t recommend them more.

        The only downside is that you can’t customize the images, and honestly, that’s something you almost need when using AI headshots. Because of that, HeadshotsByAI can feel a bit pricey since other top tools give you similar results plus editing features.

        One area where HeadshotsByAI really stands out is the lighting. I think these photos have the best lighting of any AI headshot generator I tested. The images are also very sharp, which I like.

        The main issue is you can’t fix the pictures where the AI didn’t get everything right. Sometimes the eyes look a little blank, or the lips and teeth are off.

        Still, it’s definitely worth trying!

        Secta AI is a great AI headshot generator that can turn your selfies into headshots in all sorts of styles.

        These styles range from casual selfies to formal, professional photos.

        Also, as a Finnish guy myself, I have to point out the Aurora Borealis style headshots—I thought those were amazing.

        But instead of spending more time talking it up, let me show you my experience with the tool and walk you through how to use it.

        Let me start by showing you how to create your headshots with Secta AI.

        First, head over to their homepage.

        It’ll quickly prompt you to choose a plan. This part is simple because there’s only one paid option for $49.

        Just keep in mind there’s a hidden cost—the edits cost an extra $5–$20 per month if you want to use them. I’ll explain more about that later.

        After you’ve purchased a package, you’ll set your preferences, like your gender, ethnicity, and hair color.

        Pick your headshot styles.

        You can pick the types of headshots you’d like to create from the list of options, depending on what you like in the previews. You can choose up to 20 styles for your headshots.

        Then comes the hardest part—the tool will ask you to upload a bunch of photos of yourself.

        In fact, you’ll need to upload at least 20 photos of yourself here, which is more than what most other tools require. It can take quite a bit of time to track down that many good pictures.

        One thing I liked was that this tool accepts HEIC images—I have a lot of those on my Mac for some reason. If you do too, you don’t need to convert them. Just drag and drop them right in.

        Then, after you’ve uploaded your images, go to the next screen.

        Here, the tool will ask you to pick your favorite photo:

        Once you click “Submit,” the tool starts generating your AI headshots. This part takes a bit of time. For me, it was about 3 hours from sending everything in to getting the finished photos.

        But you don’t have to sit around waiting. Just keep an eye on your email, and when you get a message like this, your headshots are ready:

        Here is my AI headshot results that I got from this tool:

        There are more than 20 images in each section, so I think I ended up with over 140 AI headshots in total.

        Here are a few more headshots that Secta created for me:

        Let me give you a closer look:

        I mean, aren’t those incredible?

        The aurora borealis image is especially great—it not only looks like me but also feels like it’s straight from my country.

        This tool also lets you edit your headshots if you want to tweak them.
